title: phaseolus vulgaris var. albidus
intro: Phaseolus vulgaris var. albidus, also known as the white kidney bean or white pea, is a type of plant that belongs to the legume family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to inhibit the action of alpha-amylase, an enzyme that breaks down carbohydrates into simple sugars, thereby reducing the absorption of starch. The health benefits of Phaseolus vulgaris var. albidus include weight loss, improved blood sugar control, and reduced risk of chronic diseases such as diabetes and cardiovascular disease. The plant can be prepared as a herbal supplement, often in the form of capsules or tablets, or as a powder that can be added to food or beverages.
